name: Justin Fields
headline: Mahomes insurance in Kansas City with rushing-floor upside
body:
Fields lands in Kansas City as the highest-leverage backup in football, and his profile remains exactly what it has always been: legs first, arm second. Across nine 2025 starts he threw for just 1,259 yards on a 62.7% completion rate, with a negative minus-0.06 EPA per play, a 44.4% success rate, and a CPOE of minus-1.9 -- bottom-tier through-air efficiency. He still salvaged 142.7 fantasy points by adding 383 rushing yards and four scores on 71 carries, the same formula that produced 289 rush yards over 10 games in 2024.

The rushing equity is the entire case here. If Mahomes opens the year limited, Fields becomes a streamable QB1 on volume alone, having posted multiple 29-plus-point outings as a starter. But the floor is real: he can disappear behind a clean passing line, and he sat at QB37 last year. He is a late-round, contingency-only stash -- buy the upside only if you can absorb the silence.
